ground_truth.title: 8 Online Interior Design Services to Try in 2024
ground_truth.author: Arricca Elin SanSone
ground_truth.main_content:
So you've decided to give your home a refresh. But where to begin? Between the hundreds of paint swatches, endless furniture arrangements, and scores of style options—translating your ideas to a tangible remodel is far from straightforward. And while you'd love to hire a bona fide interior designer to bring your dream home to life, the bill for such services can rack up fast. Consider, then, enlisting the help of an online interior design service.

In the past few years, a handful of digital decorating services has hit the market, offering personalized expertise that's as budget-friendly as it is convenient. Whether you have strong opinions about what you like or you really don't have a clue, these services can provide an action plan that ranges from a small design tweak here and there to a full-scale renovation.

Here's how it works: Most services start with a questionnaire that takes stock of design preferences, helps identify your style, and asks about your budget. You're then paired with a designer who will communicate with you through messaging or video calls. Some companies also offer in-person services in select cities. The service will then offer you a final design plan (most allow for a limited number of revisions). Once the plan is finalized, you will be connected with shopping resources that will help you find the products you liked without running all over town or aimlessly scrolling for a similar fit.

The 8 services reviewed are: 1) Havenly - $99-$159, best for budget-conscious homeowners; 2) RoomLift - $100-$1,500, best for decorators with strong ideas; 3) Decorilla - $699-$1,549, best for those needing 3D renderings; 4) Pottery Barn - Free, best for exploring options without fees; 5) The Expert - $400-$2,000, best for fans of specific designers; 6) IKEA - $99 per room, best for minimalists; 7) Stucco - $999-$1,499, best for quick turnaround; 8) Collov - $159-$259, best for time-sensitive homeowners.
with: In the past few years, a handful of digital decorating services has hit the market, offering personalized expertise that's as budget-friendly as it is convenient.
Most services start with a questionnaire that takes stock of design preferences, helps identify your style, and asks about your budget.
Havenly starts you off with a style quiz and asks about your needs for the room (from 'blank space' to 'partway there' to 'just needs love').
RoomLift is a more personable option, allowing you to work with a small team of designers.
Decorilla asks you to fill out a questionnaire about your budget and preferences.
without: Every item on this page was chosen by an ELLE Decor editor
